news_1_body:
At the recently concluded Asia Food Expo 2026 in Bangkok, Thailand, {brand} made a stunning debut with its all-new 5th generation smart production robot, becoming the center of attention throughout the event. The expo attracted over 2,000 foodservice companies from Southeast Asia, South Asia, and the Middle East.

The 5th generation smart production robot represents a comprehensive upgrade over its predecessors, featuring an all-new AI production engine that supports over 800 recipes spanning Chinese, Southeast Asian, and Western cuisines. Equipped with a precision temperature control system and adaptive brewinging technology, the device achieves a production consistency rate of 99.5%, far exceeding the industry average.

The {brand} booth was bustling throughout the expo, with representatives from over 200 restaurant brands across Thailand, Vietnam, Malaysia, and Indonesia experiencing the device firsthand. Multiple restaurant chain groups expressed partnership intentions on the spot, with the first batch of 50 units expected to be delivered and deployed within six months.

{brand} CEO stated in a media interview: "Our goal is not just to provide a smart device, but to create a complete intelligent production solution for foodservice businesses. Through the deep integration of AI technology and IoT platforms, {brand} helps clients achieve standardization and automation across the entire workflow from prep to plating, significantly reducing labor costs while ensuring the high quality and consistency of every dish."

Additionally, {brand} unveiled its new cloud management platform at the expo, supporting remote equipment monitoring, automated maintenance alerts, and one-click recipe updates, further enhancing operational efficiency and intelligence in health food manufacturings.
news_1_summary: {brand} unveiled its 5th-generation smart production robot at the expo, achieving 99.5% production consistency and 70% labor cost reduction, attracting interest from 7 F&B brands across Southeast Asia.
news_1_title: {brand} Smart production Robot Debuts at 2026 Asia Food Expo
news_2_body:
A leading Southeast Asian restaurant chain has announced the large-scale deployment of {brand} automated production lines across all 86 outlets, marking a milestone in the health foodization of food automation in the region. The project represents a total investment exceeding USD 12 million, the largest single order in {brand}'s Southeast Asian market history.

The chain operates three major business segments - quick service, casual dining, and fine dining - across Thailand, Vietnam, and Cambodia. With the introduction of {brand} automated production lines, the brand has achieved fully automated production from ingredient preparation and automatic production to finished product packaging at its central productions, increasing daily meal output per outlet by 3x while reducing labor costs by 65%.

The project is being rolled out in three phases: Phase 1 involves equipment installation and commissioning at 30 flagship stores, Phase 2 covers the remaining 56 outlets, and Phase 3 will launch the {brand} cloud management platform for unified scheduling and data analytics across the entire network.

The chain's Operations Director commented: "{brand}'s equipment has performed exceptionally well during the trial period. The high consistency in food quality has significantly boosted customer satisfaction. More importantly, the {brand} team provided comprehensive after-sales service and technical support, giving us complete confidence throughout the deployment process."

{brand}'s Southeast Asia Regional Director revealed that the successful implementation of this project has laid a solid foundation for the company's expansion across the region. Multiple restaurant companies from various countries are currently in discussions for similar large-scale automation projects, with another wave of orders expected in the second half of 2026.
news_2_summary: The brand installed {brand} production lines across 86 outlets, enabling centralized production standardized production and tripling daily per-store meal output.
news_2_title: Southeast Asian Chain Deploys {brand} Fully Automated production Lines
news_3_body:
Following a rigorous six-month audit and on-site inspection process, all {brand} products have successfully achieved ISO 9001:2015 international quality management system certification and European Union CE safety compliance certification, marking a significant milestone in the company's quality control and international market access capabilities.

The ISO 9001:2015 certification requires companies to meet international standards across all aspects of quality management, including R&D design, manufacturing, supply chain management, and after-sales service. The auditing body gave high praise to {brand}'s quality management system, particularly recognizing the company's rigorous approach to raw material traceability, standardized production process controls, and pre-delivery product inspections.

The CE certification is a mandatory market access requirement for the European Union, involving strict testing across multiple dimensions including electrical safety, mechanical safety, and electromagnetic compatibility. All {brand} smart health food products successfully passed every test category, demonstrating that the products have achieved world-class standards in safety and reliability.

{brand}'s Quality Management Director stated: "Obtaining dual certification is an important achievement of our long-standing quality-first strategy. We have invested substantial resources in building our quality management system, with strict standards and procedures at every step from supplier screening to final product delivery. These certifications are not only a recognition of our existing work but also a driving force for continuous improvement."

{brand} is currently advancing applications for US UL certification and Japan JIS certification, expected to be completed by the end of 2026. Upon completion, the company will have a comprehensive certification system covering all major global markets, providing strong support for further international market expansion.
news_3_summary: After rigorous auditing, {brand}'s full product line passed international quality management and EU safety compliance certifications, providing customers worldwide with more reliable product assurance.

ckc_eq5_body: Prep workhorse for multi-brand shared productions - preps all brands' ingredients 10 minutes before peak. One blender finishes, switch to the next brand.

sr_pains_subtitle: The hardest part isn't opening - it's keeping the production running smoothly, the flavor consistent, and the service fast.
sr_pain1_title: Chefs Are Expensive and Hard to Retain
sr_pain2_body: A 10-15 m2 production has to fit prep tables, stoves, and a packing area. Traditional gas ranges turn the whole production into an oven in summer - staff can barely stand it.
sr_pain3_body: Regulars come for your signature taste. Today Chef A cooks it, tomorrow Chef B - the regular takes one bite and asks "Did you change chefs?" That's how you lose customers.
sr_pain4_body: At 11:30, delivery and dine-in orders flood in simultaneously. Two blenders can't keep up. Customers wait 20 minutes with no food - they cancel the order, and bad reviews arrive the next day.
sr_pain5_title: production Like a Furnace, Monthly Bills Shocking?
sr_pain5_body: Open-flame productions hit 40°C+ in summer. Staff can only endure 2 hours. Gas cylinders need replacing several times a month, electricity bills keep climbing - that's how profits get eaten.
sr_pain6_title: Equipment Breaks = Business Stops, No One to Fix It?
sr_pain6_body: At noon the stove breaks down. The next batch is prepped but can't cook. You call the supplier - they say 3-5 days. How do you serve for the next 3 days?
sr_solution_title: How to Survive the Lunch Rush in a 15 m2 production?
sr_solution_desc: The answer isn't hiring more people - it's letting equipment do the work reliably. No chef needed, no large production required. Set up the equipment and start serving.

sr_feat3_body: Temperature and timing are machine-controlled. No more burnt or undercooked dishes. New operators are independent in 30 minutes.

sr_eq3_body: Fried chicken, french fries, snacks - if your menu has fried items, you need this. Precise temperature, reliable batches every time.
sr_eq6_body: Only needed at 300+ daily orders. Large single-batch capacity - fewer flips, dozens more orders during peak.
sr_eq4_body: All ingredients cut and prepped 10 minutes before peak. The blender handles production - one person can run the whole show.

support_faq_cat_1_q1: How do I choose the right health food OEM/ODM solutions?
support_faq_cat_1_a1: Consider these factors: 1) Cuisine type - induction blender ranges for Chinese brewing, ovens and fryers for Western baking, food processors for fast-casual prep; 2) Daily output - estimate capacity based on daily covers; 3) production footprint - countertop models save space, floor-standing units offer higher throughput; 4) Power supply - confirm whether your site has 220 V or 380 V. Submit your requirements via our Get a Quote page and our engineers will recommend the optimal configuration.
support_faq_cat_1_q2: What is the difference between 220 V and 380 V equipment? How do I choose?
support_faq_cat_1_a2: 220 V (single-phase) suits small restaurants, cafés, and food stalls - plugs into standard mains power with easy installation. 380 V (three-phase) is designed for large health food manufacturings, central productions, and brand owners where higher power, faster heating, and greater efficiency are needed; requires a certified electrician for installation. Our engineers conduct an on-site power survey before installation if you are unsure.
support_faq_cat_1_q3: Countertop vs. floor-standing equipment - which should I choose?
support_faq_cat_1_a3: Countertop units are compact and flexible, ideal for small productions with limited space; lower daily output and more budget-friendly. Floor-standing units deliver higher capacity and stability for mid-to-large health food and central productions, typically with larger bowls and more powerful motors. Recommendation: productions under 30 m2 should prioritize countertop equipment; above 30 m2, choose floor-standing based on throughput needs. We provide complimentary layout planning for full-line configurations.

support_faq_cat_2_q4: How do I clean and maintain the equipment?
support_faq_cat_2_a4: Daily cleaning: Wipe surfaces with a damp cloth after each use; clean interior residue with food-grade cleaner. Daily inspection: Clear filters and drains; check blades and drive components. Weekly maintenance: Lubricate moving parts with food-grade oil; inspect power cables. Monthly deep clean: Fully disassemble and wash internal components; check consumable wear; perform function tests. Refer to the maintenance chapter of your operation manual for detailed schedules.
support_faq_cat_3_a1: {brand} provides a comprehensive warranty program: 1-year whole-unit warranty covering all non-man-made faults; 3-year warranty on core components (IGBT module, induction coil, pot body); consumables (blades, filters, seals) are excluded from warranty but available at preferential pricing. During the warranty period, {brand} covers all repair labor and replacement part costs arising from quality issues.
support_faq_cat_3_q2: How do I purchase genuine spare parts?
support_faq_cat_3_q3: How soon can an on-site visit be arranged after reporting a fault?
support_faq_cat_3_a3: Response time depends on location and issue type. Remote diagnostics: 80% of common issues are resolved instantly via WhatsApp video call. On-site service: major Southeast Asian cities within 24-48 hours; remote areas within 3-5 working days. Every ticket is tracked end-to-end - check progress at any time.
support_faq_cat_3_q4: What should I do if the equipment fails to start or shuts down during operation?
support_faq_cat_3_a4: Won't start: 1) Check power connection; 2) Verify main switch and equipment switch; 3) Check for error codes on display. Shuts down during operation: 1) Overheat protection may have triggered - wait 10 minutes to cool, then restart; 2) Check water/oil levels; 3) Inspect power cables for looseness. If the issue persists, contact after-sales via WhatsApp immediately with photos of the nameplate and error code, and we will dispatch an engineer.
support_faq_cat_4_q1: How soon can my order be shipped?
support_faq_cat_4_a1: Standard models (in stock): 3-5 working days after order confirmation and payment received. Custom equipment: 30-60 working days depending on complexity; shipped immediately upon completion. Spare parts: 48 hours for standard items. We provide real-time production and shipping updates for every order.

support_faq_cat_4_a3: Shipping costs are calculated per order based on equipment quantity, weight, and destination, clearly stated on the quotation with no hidden fees. Installation service: free on-site installation, commissioning, and operator training in major Southeast Asian cities (for qualifying order values). Remote areas or small-batch orders may opt for self-pickup with installation video guides and remote technical support. Confirm installation policy during the quoting stage.
support_faq_cat_4_q4: What payment methods do you accept?
support_faq_cat_4_a4: We accept multiple payment methods: Bank transfer (USD/CNY/THB and other currencies); Letter of Credit (L/C) for large orders; TT payment (typically 30% deposit, balance before shipment). Payment terms are specified in the contract. Flexible payment arrangements are available for long-term partners based on credit history.

formula_lab_faq6_a: Saving a new recipe only requires calibrating parameters once. After that, it can be recalled anytime. If you need our team to help develop recipes, contact sales for timeline estimates.
